methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $129M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
GITAI, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $129M to date, is valued through triangulation against a peer set of nine same-sector companies spanning Series B through Series D stages. The comparable universe includes both private firms—such as 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Exotec ($2.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), and Hadrian ($1.6B)—and one public company, with valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B.

Two methods anchor the valuation. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x multiplier to GITAI's $129M total capital raised, yielding $219M. Peer-median analysis identifies $1.6B as the median valuation across the nine-company comparable set, reflecting typical Series B–D robotics funding stage valuations. The peer range spans $300M at the low end to $10.0B at the high end, indicating substantial dispersion driven by technology differentiation and go-to-market traction.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $186M (floor, anchored to stage progression) through $1.8B (ceiling, anchored to peer median with upside scenario). The median implied value across methods is $910M. Key uncertainties include GITAI's relative technical maturity and revenue traction compared to peers at similar stages, as well as capital efficiency relative to the $10.0B outlier (1X Technologies) and the tighter clustering around $1.6–$2.0B.
